description_long: This flower cluster features a central, open blossom surrounded by numerous unopened buds. The main flower displays elongated, pointed petals that are white with pinkish tips and a greenish base, radiating symmetrically around a prominent green and pink central disk. The unopened buds are bulbous, ribbed, and exhibit a gradient of pale green to pink hues, giving the cluster a textured and layered appearance. The stamens and pistils are visible in the center, with some extending outward, adding to the intricate detail. The structure is supported by a thick, green stem, and no leaves are visible in the cluster. The overall appearance is both delicate and robust, making it a striking feature in any urban garden or park setting.
description: A cluster of pale green and pink flower buds with a central, star-shaped open blossom and visible stamens, all supported by a thick green stem.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open central flower with radiating petals and surrounding unopened buds.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the rounded, ribbed buds and the cluster's dense arrangement.
description_view_2: Rear view dominated by the unopened buds and the supporting stem, with minimal visibility of the open flower.
description_view_3: Angled view showing both the open flower and the surrounding buds, with some stamens visible.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, star-like pattern around the central disk, with clear symmetry and slight overlapping at the base. The arrangement is whorled, with petals radiating outward evenly.
